presentation-quality.requirement: Specifies currency units and fiscal years/date ranges for financial and operational metrics (e.g., CHF 55m 2019, FY2023/24, 2008-2013)
presentation-quality.explanation: The response includes explicit date ranges/years (e.g., 2018–2024, 2018/19, 2023/24, 2024/25, since 2019) and specifies currency units for some metrics (e.g., EUR 1 billion, $1.90/day, $113 million, $100 million, $13 million, $12.3 million, $72 to $239).

However, the Olam/ofi section is not present in the provided text, so the structure is not applied consistently across all three companies.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains objective business analysis vocabulary without marketing language, advocacy, or informal expressions (no "crushing it," "world-class")
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses marketing/advocacy language such as “ambitious,” “leading,” “signature initiatives,” “gold standard,” and “traceability leadership,” which goes beyond objective business analysis vocabulary.

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same information or conclusion multiple times using different wording without adding new details or analysis
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeatedly restates similar high-level claims (e.g., “traceability leadership,” “vertically integrated,” “regulatory alignment/readiness,” and being a “preferred supplier” for premium contracts) in multiple sections without adding materially new data each time. It also reiterates EUDR readiness/compliance themes using different wording across bullets and paragraphs, creating redundancy rather than additional analysis.
